name: one-mcp
description: A centralized reverse-proxy platform for MCP servers — manage, group, and export as Skills from a single endpoint.

commit.message: docs: simplify features section for faster access to quick start

refactor(release): manage Homebrew formula directly in tap repo

Stop copying Formula/one-mcp.rb from this repository during release and patch the formula directly in burugo/homebrew-tap, then remove the local formula file.

fix(formula): set service PATH for npx and uvx discovery

Populate PATH in the Homebrew service environment so launchd-started one-mcp can find tool launchers like npx and uvx without requiring absolute command paths.

fix(ci): use supported macOS runner for darwin amd64 build

Switch darwin amd64 release builds to macos-latest so the CGO-enabled artifact job can run and unblock release creation.

fix(release): build darwin binaries with cgo for sqlite support

Run darwin builds on macOS runners with CGO enabled so released binaries can use go-sqlite3 at runtime, then bump VERSION to v1.0.6.

fix(formula): avoid method calls inside Homebrew service block

Resolve data directory and port directly within post_install/service blocks so Homebrew service generation does not fail with undefined formula methods.

fix(ci): remove invalid secrets reference in workflow condition

Drop the top-level `secrets` check from update-homebrew job `if` expression so GitHub Actions can parse and trigger the workflow again.

chore(release): bump version to v1.0.5

Update VERSION file to v1.0.5 in preparation for tagging and release automation.

feat(proxy): upgrade mcp-go to v0.44.0 and remove session workaround

Drop the local invalid-session 400→404 wrapper now that mcp-go returns 404, add regression coverage for group invalid-session behavior, and align Homebrew/config defaults to use one-mcp.db without service env overrides.

feat(config): auto-create runtime config file and drop UPLOAD_PATH

Bootstrap `~/.config/one-mcp/config.ini` on first start to keep JWT secrets stable across restarts, remove deprecated `UPLOAD_PATH` wiring from runtime/Homebrew/docs, and add compose config mounting guidance.

feat(homebrew): add brew install and services support for macOS/Linux

Embed locales into the binary so Homebrew service runs reliably outside the source tree, and add formula/CI updates for macOS+Linux artifacts with configurable service ports. Update English and Chinese README quick start and installation docs to cover Homebrew usage and port conflict handling.
fix(api): return JSON-RPC 2.0 error format for MCP endpoints
fix(frontend): sync API key refresh across UI (#14)
chore(deps): bump thing to v0.1.26

chore(ci): enable CGO for windows builds

This change allows Windows builds to use CGO, which is required for certain dependencies.

Co-authored-by: factory-droid[bot] <138933559+factory-droid[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(mcp): refactor Group MCP to use mcp-go library and improve execution logic

- Replace manual JSON-RPC handling with github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go library
- Implement search_tools and execute_tool as aggregate MCP tools
- Optimize search_tools response format for Cursor/Codex compatibility
- Ensure execute_tool faithfully proxies upstream structuredContent and isError flags
- Add Requests Per Day (RPD) limit enforcement to protect upstream services
- Add detailed execution logging (duration, client agent) and success-only statistics
- Refactor shared utilities to common/ and library/proxy/ for better organization
- Fix session error handling (400->404) for mcp-go streamable server
- Update tests to reflect new response structures and session initialization
- Fix run.sh to only kill LISTENING processes on the backend port

fix(docker): upgrade Node.js from 20.x to 22.x to fix npx bin resolution

npm 10.8.x (bundled with Node.js 20.x) has a bug that fails to resolve
bin entries for some packages like exa-mcp-server. Node.js 22.x includes
npm 10.9+ which fixes this issue.

Co-authored-by: factory-droid[bot] <138933559+factory-droid[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(proxy): return 404 instead of 400 for invalid MCP session IDs

Per MCP specification, invalid/expired session IDs should return
HTTP 404 Not Found so clients can detect and re-initialize sessions.
mcp-go incorrectly returns 400, so we wrap the handler to fix this.

The wrapper:
- Only buffers small responses (<512 bytes) to check for session errors
- Implements http.Flusher for SSE streaming support
- Switches to passthrough mode for large responses and SSE streams

Ref: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-03-26/basic/transports#session-management

Co-authored-by: factory-droid[bot] <138933559+factory-droid[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(proxy): force gzip encoding for upstream mcp requests

Downstream MCP clients may send Accept-Encoding with br/zstd. Go skips
auto-decompression when Accept-Encoding is set, which leads to gzip
payloads being parsed as JSON. This change forces upstream requests to
use gzip, and fails fast if the gzip reader cannot be created.

This reduces decoding errors and avoids partially-consumed response
bodies on gzip failures.

fix(proxy): auto-decompress gzip responses from upstream MCP servers

Go's http.Transport only auto-decompresses when the request does NOT
have Accept-Encoding set. When downstream clients (e.g. CherryStudio)
set Accept-Encoding, upstream may return gzip but Go won't decompress,
causing mcp-go JSON parsing to fail with 'invalid character 0x1f'.

Added gzipDecompressTransport to detect Content-Encoding: gzip and
decompress responses before passing to mcp-go.

fix(ui): add scrollbar for tools list when more than 10 tools

- Add max-h-[60vh] to tools container to enable scrolling
- Ensure proper padding for ScrollArea content

chore(docker): update Node.js installation and improve service context handling

- Added Node.js 22.x installation for better compatibility in the Dockerfile.

chore(docker): update base image and version to v0.2.3

- Changed the base image from `ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:bookworm-slim` to `astral/uv:python3.13-bookworm-slim` for improved compatibility.
- Updated the version number from `v0.2.2` to `v0.2.3` to reflect the latest changes.

fix(proxy): Improve context handling during MCP server creation

fix(handler, proxy): Enhance service toggle functionality and health checks

- Improved the ToggleMCPService function to handle service state more robustly, including error handling for service registration and unregistration.
- Added checks to prevent auto-restarting disabled services in the ServiceManager, ensuring that only enabled services are managed.
- Updated health check logic in MonitoredProxiedService to skip re-initialization for disabled services, enhancing system stability and reliability.

This commit improves the overall service management process, ensuring better handling of service states and reducing potential errors during service operations.

fix(handler): Improve service unregistration handling and logging

- Enhanced the UninstallService function to unregister services from the ServiceManager for all service types, not just stdio.
- Improved error handling to log specific cases, such as when a service is not found, and ensured that health cache is cleared regardless of unregistration success.
- Updated GetEnabledServices and GetInstalledServices functions to filter out soft-deleted services, improving data integrity in service retrieval.

This commit enhances the robustness of service management and improves logging clarity for better operational insights.

fix(proxy): Improve error message formatting in MCP service creation

- Updated error handling in the createActualMcpGoServerAndClientUncached function to ensure error messages are formatted consistently using fmt.Errorf("%s", errMsg).
- This change enhances clarity in error reporting, aiding in debugging and operational monitoring for MCP services.

feat(logging): Enhance logging capabilities for MCP services

- Added comprehensive logging for user-specific and global MCP instance creation failures, including detailed error messages saved to the database.
- Implemented logging for SSE and HTTP handler creation failures, ensuring that errors are captured and logged appropriately.
- Introduced logging for installation task submissions, starts, successes, and failures, providing better visibility into the installation process.
- Added new routes for accessing MCP logs in the API, secured with JWT and admin authentication.
- Updated frontend translations to include new log-related keys, enhancing user experience in the logs section.

This commit improves error tracking and monitoring for MCP services, facilitating better debugging and operational insights.

feat(proxy): always start stdio services on registration regardless of startup strategy

- Remove startup strategy check in RegisterService() method to ensure all stdio services start during system initialization
- Remove strategy check in ServiceFactory() to create active MCP instances for all stdio services
- Maintain on-demand logic for idle timeout and auto-restart behaviors
- Addresses user feedback: enables automatic pre-installation of MCP servers on container rebuild

feat: Update translations and enhance Edit Service Modal functionality

- Added new translation keys for "Edit" and "Edit Service" in both English and Chinese translation files to support the updated Edit Service Modal.
- Enhanced the Edit Service Modal to include a command line display for stdio services, improving user experience by providing a merged command line view.
- Updated the ServiceConfigModal to display the service's display name or name dynamically, ensuring better clarity in the UI.
- Removed the name field from the update payload in ServicesPage, streamlining the service update process.

This commit improves localization and user interface consistency across the application.

feat: Add Edit Service Modal and enhance service update functionality

- Introduced a new Edit Service Modal for updating MCP service configurations, allowing users to modify service details such as name, display name, description, command, and environment variables.
- Enhanced the UpdateMCPService function to handle background service restarts when configuration changes are detected, improving responsiveness and user experience.
- Updated the ServicesPage component to integrate the Edit Service Modal, enabling users to edit services directly from the services list.
- Improved error handling and user feedback during service updates, ensuring clear communication of success or failure.
Fixes #1

feat: Implement on-demand startup strategy for stdio services

- Added support for a new startup strategy for stdio services, allowing them to start on demand or at boot.
- Updated the UpdateOption function to validate the startup strategy input.
- Enhanced the ProxyHandler to manage on-demand service startups and idle shutdowns based on the new strategy.
- Introduced constants for the startup strategies in the common package for better maintainability.
- Updated the PreferencesPage to allow users to select their preferred startup strategy, with corresponding UI elements and translations for both English and Chinese.
- Improved service management logic to handle idle timeouts for on-demand services, ensuring efficient resource usage.

chore: Bump version to v0.1.25 and update API response handling

- Updated the version in the VERSION file from v0.1.24 to v0.1.25.
- Modified the InstallOrAddService function to return a 200 OK response when missing environment variables, indicating user action is required instead of an error.
- Increased global API and web rate limits to 500 requests and extended their duration to 5 minutes for improved performance.
- Enhanced the ServiceConfigModal to include new copy functionality for endpoint URLs and JSON configurations, improving user experience.
- Updated translation files for English and Chinese to reflect new copy actions and messages, ensuring consistency across languages.

refactor: Improve error handling and type safety across components

- Updated error handling in various components to use `unknown` type for caught errors, enhancing type safety and clarity.
- Simplified error handling logic by removing unnecessary variable declarations and improving message extraction.
- Adjusted the `PrivateRoute` component to use a consistent location reference for navigation.
- Refactored the `BatchImportModal`, `CustomServiceModal`, and other components to streamline error handling and improve user feedback.
- Enhanced the `ServiceConfigModal` to define a specific type for the service object, improving type safety and maintainability.

chore: Update Go workflow to create dummy frontend distribution file

- Modified the Go workflow to create a dummy `frontend/dist/index.html` file instead of a `.gitkeep` file, enhancing the setup for Go linting and testing processes by providing a basic HTML structure.
- This change ensures that the frontend distribution directory is not only initialized but also contains a placeholder file for better integration with frontend processes.

chore: Update Go workflow to ensure frontend dist directory is initialized

- Modified the Go workflow to create a dummy `frontend/dist` directory and include a `.gitkeep` file to ensure the directory is tracked in version control.
- This change enhances the setup for Go linting and testing processes by maintaining the necessary directory structure.

chore: Update Go workflow to create dummy frontend distribution directory

- Added steps to create a dummy `frontend/dist` directory for Go linting and testing processes.
- Removed the `test-frontend` job to streamline the workflow, as it is no longer needed.

feat: Enhance batch import for marketplace services

- Add support for uvx command to fetch PyPI package descriptions during import.
- Improve frontend batch import progress to display incremental updates.
- Resolve SQLite database locking issues by refactoring transaction handling to use pure Thing ORM.
- Optimize batch import to process services asynchronously, allowing continued processing upon individual installation failures.
- Implement cleanup logic to delete service records if installation fails or times out.
- Refactor package manager detection from if-else to a concise Go switch statement.

refactor: Enhance service installation process and error handling

- Updated the InstallOrAddService function to utilize i18n for error messages related to missing environment variables, improving user feedback.
- Enhanced the InstallationTask struct to include command and arguments, allowing for more flexible service installations.
- Modified the InstallNPMPackage and InstallPyPIPackage functions to accept command and arguments, streamlining package installation processes.
- Improved the CustomServiceModal component to handle auto-filling of environment variables based on API responses, enhancing user experience during service creation.

chore: Update dependencies and refine Redis integration documentation

- Bumped the version of the `github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go` dependency from v0.32.0 to v0.34.0 in go.mod.
- Updated README.md and README.zh.md to clarify Redis integration, removing references to rate limiting and emphasizing its role in distributed caching.

chore: Bump version to v0.1.21 and add custom arguments support

- Updated the version in the VERSION file from v0.1.20 to v0.1.21.
- Enhanced the InstallOrAddService function in market.go to support custom arguments for stdio services, allowing users to provide additional parameters during service installation.
- Updated the ServicesPage component to parse and send custom arguments from the frontend, improving the flexibility of service management and user experience.

refactor: Improve package name extraction logic in ServicesPage

- Enhanced the logic for extracting package names from service arguments and commands, ensuring that parameters starting with '-' are filtered out.
- Updated the handling of both arguments and command parts to improve clarity and functionality in determining the appropriate package manager and package name.
- This change aims to streamline the process of identifying package names, enhancing overall user experience and reducing potential errors.

refactor: Enhance PyPI package validation and description retrieval

- Renamed the function to validate and retrieve PyPI package information, improving clarity and functionality.
- Updated the InstallOrAddService function to utilize the new validation method, allowing for package description retrieval alongside existence checks.
- Improved error handling by returning detailed error messages for various failure scenarios during package validation.
- Added logic to use the retrieved package description when creating a new service, enhancing user feedback and service clarity.

chore: Bump version to v0.1.19 and add PyPI package validation

- Updated the version in the VERSION file from v0.1.18 to v0.1.19.
- Introduced a new function to validate the existence of PyPI packages via the PyPI API, enhancing package management capabilities.
- Updated the InstallOrAddService function to check for package existence before proceeding with installation, improving error handling for non-existent packages.
- Added new error messages for package validation in both English and Chinese locales to enhance user feedback.

chore: Bump version to v0.1.18 and enhance service uninstallation process

- Updated the version in the VERSION file from v0.1.17 to v0.1.18.
- Improved the UninstallService function in market.go to handle service unregistration with a timeout context, allowing for better management of potential delays and errors during the unregistration process.
- Added logic to skip physical uninstallation if a timeout occurs, treating the service as pending to ensure smoother operation.
- Enhanced the GetEnvVarsFromMCPConfig function in npm.go with additional logic to infer environment variables from command arguments and package names, improving the overall environment variable extraction process.

refactor: Improve request body handling in ProxyHandler

- Enhanced the ProxyHandler function to read and restore the request body for downstream processing, ensuring that the body is preserved even in case of read errors.
- Added logging for unexpected errors during body reading to aid in debugging.
- Clarified comments regarding the handling of JSON unmarshalling, emphasizing that the downstream handler is responsible for proper body parsing and error management.

feat: Embed version information and update dependencies

- Added functionality to embed the version file content into the application, allowing for dynamic version retrieval at runtime.
- Updated frontend dependencies in package-lock.json and package.json to ensure compatibility and leverage the latest features.
- Included new type definitions for axios and node to enhance type safety and development experience.

fix: resolve axios type compatibility issues for Ubuntu builds

- Replace deprecated AxiosRequestConfig with InternalAxiosRequestConfig
- Update AppAPIClient interface to use correct axios types
- Add headers existence checks to prevent undefined errors
- Ensure compatibility with axios 1.x across different OS environments

chore: Bump version to v0.1.17 and enhance build script for multi-platform support

- Updated the version in the VERSION file from v0.1.16 to v0.1.17.
- Enhanced the build.sh script to check for the existence of the VERSION file and read its content.
- Added functionality to create a release directory and build binaries for Linux (amd64), macOS (amd64), and macOS (arm64) with appropriate naming conventions.
- Improved output messages to indicate the build process and the location of generated binaries.
- Enhanced the createHTTPProxyHttpHandler function in service.go to include a heartbeat interval for the HTTP/MCP handler, preventing idle timeouts.

chore: Bump dependency version for github.com/burugo/thing to v0.1.24

- Updated the version in go.mod from v0.1.23 to v0.1.24.
- Updated the checksum in go.sum to reflect the new version.

chore: Update go.sum and replace logo image in App.tsx

- Removed outdated dependency entries for `github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go` version 0.31.0 from go.sum.
- Replaced the logo image in App.tsx with a globe icon for improved visual representation.

feat: Enhance HTTP server configuration and update frontend assets

- Updated the main.go file to create a custom HTTP server with specified timeouts and support for long-lived connections.
- Modified the setupGracefulShutdown function to gracefully shut down the HTTP server.
- Changed the favicon link in index.html from "vite.svg" to "logo.svg" for consistency.
- Replaced the globe icon in App.tsx with an image of the One MCP logo for improved branding.
chore: Update title in index.html from "Vite + React + TS" to "One MCP"

releases.name: Release v1.0.6
releases.body:
## What's Changed

- Fixed Homebrew runtime startup issue on macOS by shipping darwin binaries with CGO enabled for SQLite (`go-sqlite3`) support.
- Updated CI build matrix to run darwin builds on macOS runners and keep Linux builds with CGO disabled.
- Kept release/tap automation intact and refreshed Homebrew formula checksums for v1.0.6.

## Fixes

- `brew services start one-mcp` no longer fails with:
  - `Binary was compiled with 'CGO_ENABLED=0', go-sqlite3 requires cgo to work`

## Full Changelog

- https://github.com/burugo/one-mcp/compare/v1.0.5...v1.0.6

releases.name: Release v1.0.5
releases.body:
## What's Changed

- Upgraded `github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go` to **v0.44.0**.
- Removed local invalid-session status-code workaround (`400 -> 404`) and now rely on upstream MCP behavior.
- Updated proxy/group tests to align with invalid session **404** semantics.
- Removed Homebrew service env overrides in formula (`SQLITE_PATH`, `PORT`) and aligned runtime defaults.
- Updated default runtime config template to use `SQLITE_PATH=one-mcp.db`.

## Notes

- Invalid/expired session behavior now consistently follows MCP expectation: **404 Not Found**.
- In Homebrew service mode, relative DB path resolves under the service `working_dir`.

## Full Changelog

- https://github.com/burugo/one-mcp/compare/v1.0.4...v1.0.5

releases.name: Release v1.0.4
releases.body:
Bug Fixes
   •  Fix API key refresh not updating endpoint URLs in Group/Services pages (#14)
   •  Return JSON-RPC 2.0 error format for MCP endpoints (improves client compatibility)

releases.name: Release v1.0.2
releases.body:
This minor update improves the stability and compatibility of the Service Groups
   (Combo) feature.

   •  Improved AI Compatibility: Refactored Group MCP response formats to work better
      with AI clients like Cursor and Codex.
   •  Protocol Upgrade: Now using the mcp-go library for more robust protocol handling.
   •  Usage Tracking: Added daily request limits and detailed execution logs for Group
      MCP.
   •  Bug Fixes: Improved session error handling and backend process management.

readme_html:
One MCP

One MCP - A centralized proxy for Model Context Protocol (MCP) services

✨ Manage, monitor, and configure your MCP services from a single interface ✨

Overview

One MCP is a comprehensive management platform for Model Context Protocol (MCP) services. Acting as a centralized proxy, it lets you discover, install, configure, and monitor MCP services from various providers. Built with Go and React, it offers both powerful backend capabilities and an intuitive web interface.

Features

  • Service Management — Install, configure, and monitor MCP services (stdio / SSE / streamable HTTP) from a marketplace or custom sources
  • Service Groups — Combine multiple MCP services into a single endpoint; export as Anthropic Skills for Claude Code & Droid
  • Analytics — Track usage, request rates, response times, and system health in real time
  • Multi-User & OAuth — Role-based access control with GitHub / Google login
  • Flexible Deployment — SQLite (default) / MySQL / PostgreSQL, optional Redis, Docker ready, i18n (EN / ZH)

Service Groups

Create service groups to combine multiple MCP services and export as Skills:

Quick Start

Using Homebrew (macOS & Linux)

# Add tap
brew tap burugo/tap

# Install one-mcp
brew install one-mcp

# Start as background service (default port: 3000)
brew services start one-mcp

# Stop service
brew services stop one-mcp

If port 3000 is already in use, restart with a custom port:

ONE_MCP_PORT=3001 brew services restart one-mcp

Access the application at http://localhost:3000 (or your custom port).

Using Docker (Recommended)

# Run with Docker
docker run --name one-mcp -d \
  --restart always \
  -p 3000:3000 \
  -v $(pwd)/data:/data \
  buru2020/one-mcp:latest

# Access the application
open http://localhost:3000

Manual Installation

# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/burugo/one-mcp.git
cd one-mcp

# Set up environment
cp .env_example .env

bash ./run.sh

Default Login: Username root, Password 123456

Installation

Prerequisites

Homebrew Installation (macOS & Linux)

  • Homebrew installed

Manual Installation

  • Go: Version 1.19 or later
  • Node.js: Version 16 or later
  • Database: SQLite (default), MySQL, or PostgreSQL
  • Redis: Optional

Environment Configuration

Create a .env file from the template:

cp .env_example .env

Key configuration options:

# Server Configuration
PORT=3000

# Database (SQLite is default, MySQL and PostgreSQL are supported)
# SQLite(default)
# SQLITE_PATH=/data/one-mcp.db
# MySQL:
# SQL_DSN=root:password@tcp(localhost:3306)/one_mcp
# PostgreSQL:
# SQL_DSN=postgres://username:password@localhost/database_name?sslmode=disable

# Redis (optional, replace local cache for production environment)
REDIS_CONN_STRING=redis://localhost:6379

# GitHub API (optional, for querying npm's github homepage star count, without this, there will be rate limit issues)
GITHUB_TOKEN=your-github-token

Homebrew Installation (macOS & Linux)

# Add tap
brew tap burugo/tap

# Install one-mcp
brew install one-mcp

# Run in foreground
one-mcp --port 3000

# Or run as system service (default port: 3000)
brew services start one-mcp

# Use a custom service port when 3000 is occupied
ONE_MCP_PORT=3001 brew services restart one-mcp

Docker Deployment

# Build the Docker image
docker build -t one-mcp .

# Run with docker-compose (recommended)
docker-compose up -d

# Or run directly
docker run -d \
  --name one-mcp \
  -p 3000:3000 \
  -v ./data:/data \
  -e PORT=3000 \
  one-mcp

Manual Deployment

  1. Build the application:

    ./deploy/build.sh

  2. Run the server:

    ./one-mcp --port 3000

  3. Access the application: Open http://localhost:3000 in your browser

Configuration

Runtime Configuration (~/.config/one-mcp/config.ini)

One MCP supports runtime configuration from an INI file at:

~/.config/one-mcp/config.ini

The runtime priority is:

defaults < config file < environment variables < flags
  • defaults: built-in defaults (for example port 3000)
  • config file: values from ~/.config/one-mcp/config.ini
  • environment variables: values like PORT, SQLITE_PATH, ENABLE_GZIP
  • flags: command-line flags like --port (highest priority)

On first startup, One MCP automatically creates a minimal default config.ini.

Example config.ini:

PORT=3000
SQLITE_PATH=one-mcp.db
ENABLE_GZIP=true

Notes:

  • One MCP only reads ~/.config/one-mcp/config.ini for runtime file-based config.
  • Homebrew service values (ONE_MCP_PORT, --port) still override config.ini.

OAuth Setup

GitHub OAuth

  1. Create a GitHub OAuth App at https://github.com/settings/applications/new
  2. Set Homepage URL: http://your-domain.com
  3. Set Authorization callback URL: http://your-domain.com/oauth/github
  4. Configure in the application preferences

Google OAuth

  1. Create credentials at https://console.developers.google.com/
  2. Set Authorized JavaScript origins: http://your-domain.com
  3. Set Authorized redirect URIs: http://your-domain.com/oauth/google
  4. Configure in the application preferences

Database Configuration

SQLite (Default)

No additional configuration required. Database file is created at ./data/one-mcp.db.

MySQL

SQL_DSN=username:password@tcp(localhost:3306)/database_name

PostgreSQL

SQL_DSN=postgres://username:password@localhost/database_name?sslmode=disable

API Documentation

The application provides RESTful APIs for all functionality:

  • Base URL: http://localhost:3000/api
  • Authentication: Bearer token (obtained via login)
  • Content-Type: application/json

Key Endpoints

  • POST /api/auth/login - User authentication
  • GET /api/services - List installed services
  • POST /api/services - Install new service
  • GET /api/market/search - Search marketplace
  • GET /api/analytics/usage - Usage statistics

Development

Development Environment

# Start development servers
./run.sh

# This will start:
# - Backend server on :3000
# - Frontend dev server on :5173 (with hot reload)

Project Structure

one-mcp/
├── backend/         # Go backend code
├── frontend/        # React frontend code  
├── data/           # Database and uploads
├── main.go         # Application entry point
├── build.sh        # Production build script
└── run.sh          # Development script

Testing

# Frontend tests
cd frontend && npm test

# Backend tests
go test ./...
